blocks.content: Kit pulled the door until it was almost closed — not latched, because the latch would click — and they pressed back against the wall below the window and listened.
The voices moved through the rooms downstairs. One was talking. The other was quiet, and the quiet one was the one Kit was tracking.
Daisy's shoulder was against his arm. She was breathing slow and steady. He matched it.

blocks.speaker: Voice
blocks.content: There's a door here. Back stairs — old houses got back stairs.
Kit closed his eyes for one second. Opened them and looked at the window. Old latch. Below it: side yard, tall grass, a drop of about eleven feet.
He signaled Eli — one finger. Then Savannah — two. Both nodded. He looked at Daisy. She shook her head and pointed at him, then herself — after you, then me. He shook his back. First.
From below, the sound of footsteps starting up the back stairs.
